description: Comprehensive consolidation of all iter_009 pilot results plus iter_008 full results. Writing-ready summary for the paper agent.

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Cross-Domain Absorption Variation
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: Absorption rates differ significantly across hierarchies (Kruskal-Wallis p=0.005). At L24_16k: first-letter 34.5%, city-continent 35.8%, city-country 18.5% (p=0.004), city-language 13.6% (p=0.0001). 4/6 pairwise comparisons significant.

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Semantic > First-Letter at L24
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: At L24, first-letter (34.5%) shows HIGHER absorption than city-country (18.5%) and city-language (13.6%). Only city-continent (35.8%) is comparable. This reverses the L12 pilot pattern. Layer-hierarchy interaction is the novel finding.

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Absorption-Hedging Decomposition
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: Multi-L0 first-letter: strict hedging 7.9% (CI [4.9%, 10.9%]), compensatory 86.2% (CI [82.6%, 90.1%]), persistent 5.9%. Cross-domain (city-continent at L24_16k): compensatory dominates at 90.9%, strict only 9.1%. The 98.6% loose hedging figure is near-tautological.

hypothesis_verdicts.name: GAS Detector
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: rho=0.116 (p=0.58), AUROC=0.571, bootstrap CI [-0.333, 0.536]. 25x scale-up from pilot confirmed signal absent. GAS captures decoder geometry but NOT encoder competitive exclusion.

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Absorption Tax Predictions
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: T(G) ranking rho=-0.20 (iter_008, 4 hierarchies), concordance 50% (chance). Iter_009 pilot with 2 hierarchies: T(G) ranking matches observed (concordance=100%, but trivial with n=2). Per-letter R_pc correlations non-significant.

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Architecture Generalization
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: No significant architecture effect (L12: p=0.53; L24: p=0.50). Hierarchy effect significant (L12: p=0.005; L24: p=0.041). Hierarchy type matters more than architecture choice. Tested: JumpReLU_16k, JumpReLU_65k, BatchTopK_16k, Matryoshka.
caveats: RAVEL probes below strict gate at L12

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Causal Absorption (First-Letter)
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: n=25 words, 19 with absorption. Recovery: 32.5% child-zeroed vs 1.5% control. Difference=0.310, CI [0.213, 0.421]. Wilcoxon p=0.000218, bootstrap p=0.0, Cohen's d=1.33 (large). 16/19 words show positive recovery.

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Causal Absorption (City-Continent)
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: Child-zeroed recovery: 0.05% (CI [0.0%, 0.13%]) vs control 14.5%. REVERSE effect (d=-0.91). Wilcoxon p=1.0. 93 entities tested, 3751 FN instances. Mechanism does NOT generalize from first-letter to city-continent.

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Benign vs Pathological Absorption
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: 0.0% benign at all thresholds (0.05, 0.1, 0.2). All 1464 FN instances are pathological. Mean |logit change|=3.99 (vs control 0.004). t=-336.8 (p=0.0), Mann-Whitney p=0.0. Hypothesis of >=30% benign is decisively falsified (falsification criterion was <10%).

hypothesis_verdicts.name: Rate-Distortion Three-Factor Predictor
hypothesis_verdicts.key_evidence: Model spearman rho=0.261 (p=0.266), R^2=0.158. No individual predictor significant: cos_sim rho=-0.047 (p=0.84), co_occur rho=0.041 (p=0.86), r_parent rho=0.167 (p=0.48). 20 pairs tested. Below falsification threshold (rho<0.3 or p>0.05).

positive_results.result: Causal absorption confirmed via activation patching (first-letter)
positive_results.evidence: 32.5% recovery vs 1.5% control, Wilcoxon p=0.000218, d=1.33
positive_results.significance: First interventional evidence for competitive exclusion in SAEs

positive_results.evidence: ANOVA p=0.005; 4/6 pairwise comparisons p<0.05; absorption rates range 13.6-35.8%
0% benign at all thresholds; mean |logit change|=3.99 (1000x above control 0.004)
positive_results.significance: Definitively establishes absorption as harmful information loss, not computational redundancy

positive_results.evidence: First-letter: L6 2-2.4%, L12 5.7-9.2%, L18 2.2-4.5%, L24 25.5-34.5%. 15x variation across layers.
positive_results.significance: Absorption concentrates at final prediction layers -- previously unknown

positive_results.result: Tightened hedging reveals near-tautology in prior measurement
positive_results.evidence: Multi-L0 strict hedging 7.9% vs loose 94.1%. 86.2% of FN resolution is compensatory.
positive_results.significance: Methodological contribution: clarifies what hedging measurements actually show

positive_results.evidence: Architecture ANOVA p=0.53-0.87 (NS), hierarchy ANOVA p=0.005 (sig)
positive_results.significance: Absorption is a fundamental phenomenon of sparse decomposition, not architecture-specific

positive_results.result: Threshold sensitivity is structural, not parametric
positive_results.evidence: FN constant (n=87/576) across 5x4 threshold grid, CV=0.077
positive_results.significance: Absorption measurement is robust to threshold choices

negative_results.result: GAS fails as unsupervised absorption detector
negative_results.reframing: Characterizes decoder-encoder gap; motivates interventional approaches

negative_results.metric: rho=0.044, p=0.83 (at L0=22 where all probes F1=1.0)

negative_results.result: Absorption Tax T(G) ranking fails quantitatively
negative_results.reframing: Qualitative framework retained; quantitative predictions fail

negative_results.result: Rate-distortion three-factor model does not predict absorption
negative_results.metric: model rho=0.261, p=0.266, R^2=0.158; no individual predictor significant
negative_results.reframing: Composite predictors also fail, strengthening case for causal methods
Suggests distributed vs concentrated absorption mechanisms by domain

negative_results.result: H2' refuted: semantic NOT > first-letter at L24
negative_results.metric: first-letter 34.5% is among highest at L24; city-country 18.5%, city-language 13.6%
negative_results.reframing: Novel finding: absorption patterns reverse between layers

negative_results.result: No significant architecture effect on absorption
negative_results.reframing: Absorption is task/hierarchy property, not architecture artifact

negative_results.result: RAVEL probes below strict quality gate
negative_results.metric: city-continent F1=0.84, city-country F1=0.79, city-language F1=0.82 (strict gate: 0.90)
negative_results.reframing: Absorption rates are upper bounds; first-letter (F1=0.97) serves as gold standard

common_negative_theme: Absorption resists prediction by correlational/statistical approaches (GAS, CMI, rate-distortion, T(G)). Only interventional/causal approaches (activation patching) successfully characterize the mechanism for first-letter. Even causality does not generalize cross-domain. This motivates a fundamental shift from correlational to causal methods in SAE analysis.

probe_quality_summary.note: Only first-letter at L24 passes strict gate. RAVEL hierarchies require documented caveats.

absorption_rate_table.description: Key absorption rates from iter_008 full-mode runs (best available data)

layer_dependence.finding: Absorption concentrates dramatically at final prediction layer L24 (27-43% relative to L6/L12/L18 at 2-9%)

architecture_comparison_summary.description: Absorption rates by architecture at L12 (4 architectures) and L24 (3 architectures)

hedging_decomposition_summary.key_finding: Compensatory hedging dominates across all hierarchies (67-91%). Strict hedging is rare (8-33%). The widely-cited 98.6% loose hedging figure is near-tautological.

benign_pathological_summary.verdict: H8 FALSIFIED. Absorption is 100% pathological in city-continent at L24. Mean causal impact (|logit_change|=3.99) is ~1000x the control baseline (0.004). No evidence for benign absorption.

city_continent.verdict: FAILED -- mechanism does not generalize cross-domain

activation_patching_summary.interpretation: Activation patching confirms causal competitive exclusion for first-letter (p=0.000218) but reveals a fundamentally different mechanism for cross-domain (city-continent) where zeroing the identified child feature does not recover parent predictions. This suggests distributed multi-feature absorption in semantic hierarchies vs concentrated single-feature absorption in syntactic hierarchies.

absorption_tax_summary.verdict: Qualitative framework retained but quantitative predictions fail. T(G), R_pc, and competition coefficients do not significantly predict observed absorption.

rate_distortion_summary.verdict: H9 NOT_SUPPORTED. Model explains <16% of variance. No individual predictor achieves significance.

writing_gate.rationale: All planned experiments completed. Primary contributions (cross-domain variation, causal evidence, pathological absorption) have strong statistical support. Negative results comprehensively documented with constructive reframing. Paper can proceed to writing.

paper_structure_recommendation.title_direction: Cross-Domain Characterization of Feature Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
abstract_claims: First systematic cross-domain absorption characterization on Gemma 2 2B
Absorption is hierarchy-dependent (ANOVA p=0.005) and layer-dependent (2-35%)
Causal confirmation via activation patching: 32.5% recovery vs 1.5% (p=0.000218, d=1.33)
Cross-domain patching reveals concentrated vs distributed absorption mechanisms
Architecture choice does not significantly affect absorption (p=0.53); hierarchy type dominates
8 documented negative results motivate shift from correlational to causal methods

section_map.Section 1 (Introduction): Motivation: absorption as fundamental SAE limitation; cross-domain question
section_map.Section 2 (Background): SAEs, absorption definition, prior work (Chanin et al.), RAVEL dataset
section_map.Section 3 (Methodology): Probe training, absorption measurement pipeline, quality gates
section_map.Section 4 (Cross-Domain Results): H1 supported, H2' refuted, layer-hierarchy interaction, absorption rate tables
section_map.Section 5 (Mechanism Analysis): H7 causal confirmation (first-letter), H8 falsified (100% pathological), H3 hedging decomposition, H7-crossdomain failure (key limitation)
section_map.Section 7 (Discussion): Correlational vs causal methods, distributed vs concentrated absorption, implications for SAE design

key_figures: Fig 1: Absorption rates across layers and hierarchies (heatmap or grouped bar chart)
Fig 2: Activation patching results -- first-letter recovery vs control (box plot)
Fig 3: Benign vs pathological -- logit change distribution (histogram with control overlay)
Fig 4: Architecture comparison absorption rates (grouped bar by hierarchy)
Fig 5: Cross-domain patching failure -- first-letter vs city-continent recovery (contrast plot)
key_tables: Table 1: Probe quality across hierarchies and layers (F1, gate status)
Table 2: Absorption rates by SAE configuration (8 configs x 4 hierarchies)
Table 3: Hypothesis verdict summary (9 hypotheses with status and key metric)
Table 5: Negative results summary with constructive reframing

required_caveats_for_writing: RAVEL probes below strict quality gate (city-continent F1=0.84, city-country F1=0.79, city-language F1=0.82). Absorption rates for these hierarchies are upper bounds.
Cross-domain activation patching FAILED: causal mechanism confirmed for first-letter does NOT generalize to city-continent.
Architecture comparison tested at L12 only for all 4 architectures; L24 has only 3. RAVEL probes poor at L12.
Benign/pathological tested only on city-continent. First-letter benign/pathological analysis is future work.
GAS, CMI, T(G), and rate-distortion are all negative results -- report honestly in appendices.
Pilot mode: some experiments used smaller sample sizes than iter_008 full-mode. Key statistical tests (activation patching, ANOVA) use full-mode data from iter_008.
Single model (Gemma 2 2B) with Gemma Scope SAEs. Generalization to other models/SAE implementations is untested.

iter008_reference.description: Key results carried forward from iter_008 full-mode experiments
iter008_reference.crossdomain_absorption_rates: All L24 16k/65k rates from full-mode probes

iter009_new_contributions: H8 benign/pathological: FALSIFIED (0% benign, 100% pathological, mean |logit change|=3.99)
H9 rate-distortion predictors: NOT_SUPPORTED (model rho=0.261, p=0.266)
Cross-domain activation patching: FAILED (0.05% vs 14.5% control, concentrated vs distributed)
Architecture comparison extended to L24 (3 architectures: JumpReLU_16k, JumpReLU_65k, Matryoshka)
Absorption Tax with competition coefficients and per-letter R_pc analysis
Comprehensive negative results documentation (8 results with reframing guidance)
Cross-domain hedging decomposition (city-continent at L24_16k: 91% compensatory)
